description: SymPy galoistools core: univariate polynomials over the prime field GF(p), represented as big-endian coefficient lists of Nat (head = leading coefficient, [] = zero polynomial; all coefficients reduced modulo p, no leading zeros). Translated from sympy/polys/galoistools.py. Two modules. Ring (gfAdd/gfSub/gfMul/gfNeg/gfMonic): the GF(p)[x] ring operations, pinned by a commutative-group additive structure (add commutativity/associativity/identity, additive inverse, subtraction=add-of-negation) and a multiplicative structure anchored SEMANTICALLY through the frozen evaluation map polyEval (the ring homomorphism GF(p)[x] -> GF(p) at a point): multiplication is characterized by the evaluation homomorphism polyEval (gfMul f g) x = (polyEval f x * polyEval g x) % p, from which commutativity/associativity/distributivity follow at every field point. gfMonic is pinned to producing a leading coefficient of 1 (leadCoeff (gfMonic f).2 = 1 when the leading coefficient is a unit). Division (gfDiv/gfRem/gfQuo/gfGcd/gfPowMod): the headline gfDiv is Euclidean division f = q*g + r, characterized by the recomposition identity gfAdd (gfMul q g) r = f over a normalized monic divisor together with the remainder degree bound (r = [] or deg r < deg g); gfRem/gfQuo are its projections; gfGcd is the MONIC gcd, pinned to divide both inputs (gfRem f gcd = [] and gfRem g gcd = []), to be MAXIMAL (every common divisor of f and g divides the gcd), to be monic (leadCoeff = 1 when nonzero), to be empty exactly at the double-zero input, and to equal the monic associate at the zero and self boundaries; gfGcdex is extended Euclid, pinned by the BEZOUT identity — writing (s, t, h) = gfGcdex f g p, the cofactors recompose the gcd as gfAdd (gfMul s f) (gfMul t g) = h with h = gfGcd f g (over a prime field with normalized inputs) — plus its double-zero and left-zero boundary laws; gfPowMod is modular exponentiation by repeated squaring, pinned by the zero-exponent unit, the recurrence f^(n+1) mod g = (f^n mod g * f) mod g, and reducedness of the result. All specs anchored on frozen operations (gfStrip, gfDegree, leadCoeff, gfTrunc, polyEval and the frozen ring ops) so each pins a unique answer; the field laws that require an invertible modulus carry a frozen PrimeField p hypothesis (1 < p with trial-division primality), since SymPy's GF(p) requires a prime modulus. Everything is discrete Nat/Int over %, *, +, -; no Float. invMod is total/terminating via a fuel-bounded extended Euclid; division/gcd/powmod loops are fuel-bounded. Scope: the benchmark models the ring operations, Euclidean division with its identity + uniqueness + degree bound, the monic gcd divide-both/maximality/normalization/boundary laws, extended-Euclid Bezout (gfGcdex: s*f + t*g = h = gfGcd), and modular exponentiation recurrence/reducedness. Full modular-exponentiation multiplicativity (f^n mod g as a semantic power via polyEval) is OUT of scope (mathlib-scale ring-theory proof).
